overview.summary: Business is loss-making at -13.8% net margin. Advertising is the #1 expense at ₹22.7L (59% of total expenses) per Tally books. Extreme product concentration — Gingham Grace dress = 51.5% of all revenue. Bank account hit ₹0 six times. Cash crisis is real and urgent.

profitability.advisory: Gross margin is healthy at 74.2% but net is -13.8%. The ₹22.7L advertising expense (per Tally) is the primary profit killer — 59% of total expenses. AOV is ₹1,737. Tally-based ROAS is only 1.92x (₹43.5L sales / ₹22.7L ads).
profitability.dataGap: COGS per product not configured in Shopify — pending from client. All Shopify COGS fields show ₹0. Using Tally aggregate COGS for now.

revenue.advisory: ₹10.3L gap between Shopify (₹53.8L) and Tally (₹43.5L) revenue needs investigation. Could be timing differences, COD non-delivery, or unrecorded returns. Sep–Nov peak (₹28L) accounted for 48% of annual revenue — high seasonality risk.

products.concentrationSub: Gingham Grace family = 51.5% of all revenue
products.deadStockAlert: 23 products with >5 units in stock and 0–2 sold in 9 months. Estimated capital tied up: ₹2–3L. These SKUs are blocking working capital and warehouse space.
products.advisory: CRITICAL: Gingham Grace dress = 51.5% of all revenue. Single-product dependency this extreme is a business survival risk. Need to diversify urgently. 23 dead stock SKUs are tying up capital with near-zero turns.

payments.rtoAlert: 817 COD orders were fulfilled (shipped) but remain unpaid = ₹16.5L at risk of RTO (Return to Origin). COD cancellation rate is 4.2% vs Prepaid 0.8% — 5x higher. Without Shiprocket RTO data, actual losses cannot be quantified.
payments.unfulfilledAlert: 354 orders remain unfulfilled, of which 159 are paid but not shipped. This represents potential revenue leakage and customer satisfaction risk.
payments.advisory: COD cancellation is 5x prepaid. ₹16.5L in RTO risk exposure. PrepaidDiscount (₹100 off) is working well — drives 47% prepaid share. Consider increasing to ₹150 to shift the COD–prepaid ratio further. Need Shiprocket RTO report to quantify actual losses.

marketing.advisory: Tally-based ROAS at 1.92x is well below the 3x minimum for profitability. Tally books show ₹22.7L ad expense (incl. GST). Bank shows only ₹6.93L in direct Meta debits, while Meta's own report shows ₹25.8L (Apr–Feb, 11 months). AOV is ₹1,737. CBO campaigns outperform ABO by 32% — shift budget accordingly.
marketing.dataGap:
Meta Ads detailed data pending. To enable campaign-level insights, we need the following exports from Meta Ads Manager:

1. Campaign Performance Report — Date range: Apr 2025 to present. Columns: Campaign Name, Campaign ID, Objective, Daily Spend, Impressions, Reach, Clicks (Link), CTR, CPM, CPC, Purchases, Purchase Value, ROAS.
2. Ad Set Level Report — Same date range. Columns: Ad Set Name, Audience Type (Lookalike/Interest/Custom), Daily Spend, Results, Cost per Result, Frequency.
3. Ad Creative Report — Columns: Ad Name, Format (Image/Video/Carousel), Spend, Engagement Rate, Purchases, ROAS.
4. Audience Insights — Age, Gender, Location breakdown of purchasers.

Export path: Meta Ads Manager → Ads Reporting → Export → CSV. Select 'All columns' and date range Apr 1, 2025 to present.

customers.advisory: 9.4% repeat rate is critically low for D2C (benchmark 20–25%). Only 30 customers ordered 3+ times. Zero retention strategy visible — no email marketing, no loyalty program. LTV is essentially single-order.
customers.dataGap: Customers Export not yet shared — needed for cohort analysis and LTV calculation.

geography.advisory: Bangalore alone = 16% of orders. Top 5 cities = 39%. High metro concentration means opportunity in Tier 2/3 cities. Karnataka + Maharashtra = 40% of orders — concentrate logistics partnerships here for faster delivery and lower shipping costs.

cashflow.zeroAlert: The business bank account (PNB) reached exactly ₹0.00 on 6 dates between Oct 2025 and Jan 2026: 02-Oct, 14-Dec, 20-Dec, 04-Jan, 05-Jan, 06-Jan. The balance dropped below ₹1,000 on 10 occasions. Business is technically insolvent at operating level.
cashflow.advisory: Cash position is emergency-level. ₹7.51L in self-transfers out of the business account, ₹6L to NOWDEZ, ₹6.93L to Meta Ads. Balance hit zero 6 times. ₹34.5L in unpaid vendor bills with near-zero bank balance = imminent cash crisis. Need to: 1) Negotiate NOWDEZ payment plan 2) Reduce Meta daily spend caps 3) Separate personal from business spending (₹42K in food delivery + personal).

notifications.title: Cash Crisis Alert
notifications.desc: Bank account hit ₹0 six times. Immediate attention required.

notifications.title: High Product Concentration
notifications.desc: Gingham Grace = 51.5% of revenue. Diversification needed.

notifications.title: Ad Spend Review
notifications.desc: Meta ads ₹22.7L with ROAS 1.92x. Below breakeven target of 3x.

insights.title: Loss-Making Business
insights.body: Net margin -13.8%. Advertising at ₹22.7L (per Tally) is 59% of total expenses. Tally-based ROAS is only 1.92x. The business is bleeding cash with every order.
actions: Reduce advertising spend and reallocate to high-ROAS campaigns only
Target ROAS of 4x+ before scaling any campaign
Reduce non-essential opex by 20% immediately

insights.title: Cash Crisis
insights.body: Bank balance hit ₹0 six times. ₹34.5L unpaid to vendors with near-zero cash reserves.
actions: Negotiate 90-day payment plan with NOWDEZ for ₹27.8L outstanding (81% of ₹34.5L payables)
Stop ₹7.5L+ self-transfers from business account — separate personal finances
Build 30-day cash reserve target of ₹3L minimum

insights.title: Single Product Dependency
insights.body: Gingham Grace dress = 51.5% revenue. One product failure = business collapse.
actions: Launch 3–5 new products in the ₹1,500–2,500 range within 60 days
Allocate 30% of ad budget to non-Gingham products
Set maximum product concentration target of 40%

insights.title: RTO Exposure
insights.body: ₹16.5L in COD orders at risk. COD cancel rate 5x prepaid.
actions: Increase prepaid discount from ₹100 to ₹150 to shift COD ratio
Implement COD verification (IVR/WhatsApp confirmation) before dispatch
Get Shiprocket RTO report to quantify actual losses

insights.title: Revenue Reconciliation Gap
actions: Reconcile Shopify orders with Tally sales entries month-by-month
Identify COD non-deliveries not recorded as returns
Set up automated Shopify-Tally sync process

insights.title: Below-Par ROAS
insights.body: Tally-based ROAS 1.92x vs 3x minimum. ₹22.7L ad expense (per Tally) against ₹43.5L sales. AOV ₹1,737 with 0.82% conversion rate.
actions: Pause all campaigns with ROAS below 2x
Shift budget from ABO to CBO campaigns (32% better performance)
Test lookalike audiences from top 255 repeat customers

insights.title: Dead Inventory
insights.body: 23 SKUs (₹2–3L tied up) with near-zero sales in 9 months.
actions: Run clearance sale at 50–60% off for dead stock within 30 days
Stop reordering any SKU with <5 units sold in 3 months
Free up ₹2–3L in working capital from dead inventory liquidation

insights.title: Low Repeat Rate = Retention Upside
insights.body: 9.4% vs 20–25% benchmark. Email marketing + loyalty program can double LTV.
actions: Set up automated post-purchase email flow (day 1, 7, 30)
Launch simple loyalty program with points on repeat purchases
Target 15% repeat rate within 6 months — worth ₹5–8L incremental revenue

pending: Meta Ads — Campaign Performance Report (daily spend, ROAS, purchases by campaign)
Meta Ads — Ad Set Report (audience targeting, frequency, cost per result)
Meta Ads — Ad Creative Report (format, engagement, ROAS per ad)
Meta Ads — Audience Insights (age, gender, location of purchasers)
